summary.intent: Fix three bugs (ku8, ku9, ku-) discovered by Codex review pass 7 in the file upload state machine — a stale-queue callback bug, a drag-state leak after upload completion, and a missing distinction between drop vs. selection in accessibility announcements.
summary.outcome: All three bugs fixed with TDD: 145 tests pass, clippy clean, 90.06% coverage with 0 uncovered lines, spec updated and validated, changes committed and pushed as pass-7.

repo: The project runs iterative Codex review 'passes' that produce numbered bug threads (ku8, ku9, ku-); each pass builds on findings from previous passes.
Spec files in spec/components/specialized/ are kept in sync with implementation changes and validated as part of the commit workflow.
Coverage is measured and must stay at 0 uncovered lines; the threshold is tracked per-pass.
Clippy and rustfmt are enforced before committing — `.to_vec()` on `&Vec` must be `.clone()` per clippy's lint.

The file upload state machine uses plan helpers (`upload_finish_plan`, `file_queue_plan`, `apply_selected_files_plan`) and effect helpers (`files_change_effect`) that thread context through all state transitions.

code.finding: ku8: `files_change_effect` was capturing the queue snapshot at call time (during `StartUpload`, when items are `Uploading`), but the `FilesChanged` callback is meant to report the final post-drain queue. Fix: read the queue from ctx at run time inside the effect closure, not from the caller's snapshot.

ku9: `upload_finish_plan` and `file_queue_plan` were transitioning to `Idle` unconditionally, even when dragging was active, leaving `data-ars-dragging` stuck. Fix: added `dragging: bool` parameter — finish to `DragOver` when dragging is true.

ku-: `AnnounceFilesAdded` effect was emitted identically for drop and file-selection, but the spec requires drop=assertive vs. selection=polite. Fix: added `from_drop: bool` field to the effect variant and threaded it through `apply_selected_files_plan` and its call sites.
Existing drop-during-upload test assertion for `AnnounceFilesAdded` needed `from_drop: true` added after the enum variant gained the field.
Existing selection and drop_announces tests needed `from_drop: false` and `from_drop: true` respectively after the enum variant change.

workflow: TDD discipline: existing test assertions are updated first to reflect the new contract, then the implementation is changed to satisfy them, then new tests are added for the specific bugs.
Spec markdown has an MD060 column-alignment lint that can be triggered by wide table cells; the fix was to shorten the cell content (using shorthand) rather than widening the whole column.
After threading a new parameter through helper functions, all call sites must be audited; the transcript shows this required multiple Read+Edit cycles across the same file.

friction: Threading `dragging` and `from_drop` parameters required reading the file multiple times to locate all call sites, resulting in several sequential Read+Edit cycles on the same file.
Clippy flagged `.to_vec()` on a `&Vec` reference — required a separate fix pass after the main implementation was done.
A recurring MD060 markdown alignment nit in the spec table needed special handling (shortening cell content) rather than a straightforward fix.
